cases.passage: As I sit in my grandmother's old armchair, surrounded by the familiar scent of freshly baked cookies and the warm glow of the fireplace, I am transported back to my childhood memories. I remember the countless afternoons I spent playing in the backyard, chasing after butterflies and watching the clouds drift lazily by. My grandmother would join me, and together we would pick wildflowers and make daisy chains. Those carefree days were a treasure trove of laughter, love, and adventure. As I grew older, I began to appreciate the value of those memories, and I now cherish the time spent with my grandmother, who was more than just a family member - she was a guardian of my childhood.

questions.q: What is the narrator transported back to as they sit in their grandmother's armchair?
questions.explanation: The narrator is transported back to their childhood memories as they sit in their grandmother's armchair.

opts: (B) They picked wildflowers and made daisy chains

questions.explanation: The narrator now appreciates the value of their childhood memories.

questions.explanation: The narrator's grandmother is more than just a family member to them.

opts: (B) Their childhood and time with their grandmother
questions.explanation: The narrator now cherishes their childhood and the time spent with their grandmother.

cases.passage: I still remember the summer holidays of my childhood, spent at my grandparents' farm. Every morning, I would wake up at the crack of dawn to help my grandfather with the chores. We would feed the cows, collect the eggs from the chicken coop, and milk the cows. My grandfather would tell me stories of his own childhood, of the struggles he faced and the triumphs he achieved. Those early morning hours were a special time, just the two of us, surrounded by the sights and sounds of nature. As I grew older, I began to appreciate the value of those lessons, and I now carry them with me as I navigate my own journey in life.

questions.q: Where did the narrator spend their summer holidays as a child?
questions.explanation: The narrator spent their summer holidays at their grandparents' farm.

questions.q: What did the narrator help their grandfather with every morning?
opts: (B) Feeding the cows and collecting eggs
questions.explanation: The narrator helped their grandfather with feeding the cows and collecting eggs every morning.

questions.explanation: The narrator's grandfather told him stories of his own childhood.

questions.explanation: The narrator's early morning hours with their grandfather were at the crack of dawn.

opts: (A) The lessons they learned from their grandfather
(B) The memories of their summer holidays
(C) The stories of their grandfather's childhood
questions.explanation: The narrator now carries the lessons they learned from their grandfather with them.
